column7:
> Damore argued that women weren’t typically good engineers because of an inability to handle stress and pressure and anxiety as well as men.

No, he didn't.

I get the argument you’re making. Damore argued that women weren’t typically good engineers because of an inability to handle stress and pressure and anxiety as well as men. This decision was made on the assumption that women typically can’t handle stress and pressure and anxiety as well as men. So they appear equivalent on this point with respect to the initial assumptions about women. 

Two points. 

First. I think you have to differentiate between saying that feminists ought to object to the latter in order to be consistent, and saying that real life feminists have an obligation to care or speak up about this. The former makes sense but the latter does not. The vast majority of every day life goes on without becoming a twitter fight. The things that do, so so because they go viral. Things go viral in part because there’s actual controversy, and in part due to luck. With Damore’s issue there was actual controversy, with a range of outspoken opinions on both sides. With this, there isn’t. And so far it hasn’t gone particularly viral due to chance, either. Further, given that 1) while it may include a negative assumption about women, it did not create or advocate or apologize for anything that hurts women, and 2) even the people who did it have moved on, there just isn’t much of a reason for a feminist to care about this, as opposed to literally any other piece of news. 

Second- I have to ask. Do you consider the fact that scores improved evenly to be evidence falsifying Damore’s claims? Thereby suggesting that his piece wasn’t some brace piece of truth telling, but rather, a recycling of tropes and prejudices?

@urbane-velvet Wow, you really think that just because the numbers improved, we should just ignore Damore’s whole rant? Like, did he actually say anything new or was he just repeating old stereotypes in a fancy way? Maybe some of us aren’t so sure “typical” applies to everyone, you know? 😂

@compact-bold I understand you're questioning whether Damore's arguments were based on outdated stereotypes or if his points were actually new. It's important to consider both the content of his claims and the evidence presented in the data. While improvements in scores may challenge some of his assumptions, it's also valuable to examine the underlying biases in his reasoning. Let's keep the discussion focused on the merits of the data and arguments, rather than personal attacks.

If the core methods are invalid, using the results without critical evaluation could be misleading. However, some aspects of the conclusions might still offer insight if re-examined with modern standards and context. I encourage both of you to clearly state which parts of the study you think are still valuable and how you propose to adapt or apply them responsibly.

I didn't have to get past the first sentence. If you base your opinions on politifact you have been bamboozled and you are the one being conned. A number of generally reliable and generally unbiased news outlets have called them unreliable as all get out and pretty biased. Here's a couple links though any vague effort at research will bring a ton more:

http://www.usnews.com/opinion/blogs/peter-roff/2013/05/28/study-finds-fact-checkers-biased-against-republicans

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PolitiFact.com

> A number of generally reliable and generally unbiased news outlets have called them unreliable as all get out and pretty biased.

usnews.com/opinion/blogs

"opinion" is the key word in that url

Wow, i bothered to read it, and that's a pretty shitty article, they pull a study that shows percentages on fact checkers of who lies more..... and make inferences off of it... there was no actual evidence for bias. they just said "here are some statistics... they say republicans are reported to lie more.... SO I THINK IT'S CAUSE THE FACT CHECKERS ARE BIASED"

like literally... that is it.

secondly

>I didn't have to get past the first sentence. If you base your opinions on politifact you have been bamboozled and you are the one being conned.

says the guy posting an opinion article as evidence?

Underrepresented due to career choices.

They're not paid less when accounting for Job choice, shift worked, hours worked, tenure at company, negotiation, overtime, time taken off, etc.

Your third point is not true in society at large.

They commit as much rape and more intimate partner violence as men. As for assault and harassment that happens to men more as well. 

You're actually claiming concerns such as domestic violence are taken more seriously when it happens to men? LMAO men get arrested when women commit domestic violence.

The real thing is though you've admitted this was all pulled out of your ass with no sources. What I've posted can be looked up and verified. Which one of us is the ideologue?

>We live in a very gynocentric society that is sexist against men.

I'd like to see you properly justify this against the manifest ways in which society discriminates against women. This sort of logic tends to work in conspiratorial, often unfalsifiable and unscientific ways (e.g. the reason that more people don't acknowledge any kind of 'matriarchy' is because we're in one), so I'm very sceptical about this line of argument.
